page: Google Cloud Platform a2-megagpu-16g is an accelerator-optimized server configured with 16 NVIDIA Ampere A100 GPUs providing a total of 640 GB of VRAM. The host system is powered by an Intel Xeon x86_64 processor with 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, operating at a base frequency of 2.2 GHz. The CPU features a 38 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and AVX-512 VNNI. System memory totals 1360.0 GB, yielding approximately 14.17 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local NVMe storage.

Benchmark evaluations reveal a distinct contrast between single-core and multi-core capabilities. Single-core performance is relatively weak, as evidenced by lower scores in stress-ng single-core, single-threaded gzip compression, and single-threaded bzip3 decompression. However, multi-core and parallel processing capabilities are exceptionally strong. The server achieves top-tier results in multi-core stress-ng, passmark CPU extended instructions, and memory bandwidth tests, with read-only small block memory bandwidth reaching 4205.12 GB/sec. Redis SET operations and static web serving throughput also rank in the top 25% of tested systems.

Given the massive GPU and memory footprint, this server represents a highly specialized hardware profile where cost efficiency is realized through massive parallelization and resource density rather than low-cost entry points. The lack of local storage requires external block storage integration, which is a key architectural tradeoff. This configuration is engineered for massive parallel workloads, including large-scale deep learning training, high-performance computing, complex simulations, and memory-intensive GPU-accelerated data science pipelines.
description: Google Cloud Platform a2-megagpu-16g is an accelerator-optimized server engineered for massive parallel computing workloads. It features 16 NVIDIA Ampere A100 GPUs with 640 GB of total VRAM, paired with an Intel Xeon x86_64 processor offering 96 vCPUs, 48 physical cores, and 1360.0 GB of system memory. The processor operates at 2.2 GHz and supports AVX-512 instruction sets, which enhances its vector processing capabilities. While single-core CPU benchmarks are relatively weak, the server delivers exceptional multi-core performance, high memory bandwidth, and strong throughput in database and web serving tests. The system does not include local storage, requiring external storage solutions. This server is highly suited for large-scale deep learning, GPU-accelerated data science, and complex scientific simulations.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform a2-megagpu-16g is an accelerator-optimized server featuring 16 NVIDIA A100 GPUs, 96 Intel Xeon vCPUs, and 1360 GB of RAM, designed for massive parallel computing and deep learning workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform a2-megagpu-16g features 16 NVIDIA A100 GPUs, 96 Intel Xeon vCPUs, and 1360 GB of RAM for GPU-accelerated parallel workloads.
tagline: An accelerator-optimized platform featuring sixteen GPUs and massive memory capacity for high-performance parallel computing and deep learning workloads.
bullet_points: Equipped with 16 NVIDIA Ampere A100 GPUs providing 640 GB of total VRAM
Powered by an Intel Xeon x86_64 processor with 96 vCPUs and 1360 GB of RAM
Delivers exceptional multi-core performance and high memory bandwidth
Lacks local storage, requiring external block storage configurations
Best suited for large-scale deep learning, simulations, and GPU-accelerated pipelines
